ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the species. In the film, Toothless struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to q1ffd1afe241 q1ffd1afe242 q1ffd1afe243 q1ffd1afe245 the dragon's diet by catching and fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that trapped, he have required 13 Icelandic cod 56 Atlantic salmon day, totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have to carry 828 fish weighing total of kg the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

He and the team were stunned when they saw these radially stretched structures. "We had to do a lot of work to establish that we weren't fooling ourselves," he said. "And we found that these filaments are not random but appear to be tied to the outflow of our black hole. I scrolled down one page in the browser.

Here is a screenshot of [There Are Hundreds of Mysterious Filaments at the Center of the Milky Way - Universe Today](https://www.universetoday.com/161812/there-are-hundreds-of-mysterious-filaments-at-the-center-of-the-milky-way/). The viewport shows 9% of the webpage, and is positioned 37% down from the top of the page.
Automatic OCR of the page screenshot has detected the following text:

We have a newsletter!  
Check out the weekly news with our Friday newsletter. It’s curated by Fraser Cain, the publisher of Universe Today.

Email Send

MeerKAT image of the galactic center with color-coded position angles of the short, radial filaments. Courtesy: Farhad Yusef-Zadeh/Northwestern University

Pinning Down Filament Histories
Like other supermassive black holes, Sgr A* has been known to swallow up material occasionally. Then, it “burps out” an energetic, highly magnetized blast of energy and plasma. That seems the most likely explanation for the appearance of the bubbles and filaments. It’s possible that such a jet collided with clouds of gas and dust in the immediate neighborhood. It could also help hint at some origins of other more subtle clouds.
